steps.title: Identify the student’s goal
steps.content:
The goal is to compare the sizes of the two mountain ranges. To do that, the answer must name both ranges and state which is larger, ideally with exact measurements. The notes provide: Peloncillo-Pan Duro = 1,360 mi²; Sierrita = 63 mi².
  • Choice A — There are dozens of isolated mountain ranges located in the southwestern US and northwestern Mexico.: States the number of sky islands in the region — never mentions sizes or compares the two ranges. ✗
  • Choice B — In terms of area, the Peloncillo-Pan Duro Mountains (which cover 1,360 mi²) are larger than the Sierrita Mountains (63 mi²).: Directly compares both ranges by area with exact measurements. ✓
  • Choice C — Sky islands in northwestern Mexico and the southwestern US can vary in size.: Makes a vague claim about size variation across all sky islands — never compares these two specific ranges. ✗
  • Choice D — The Peloncillo-Pan Duro Mountains and the Sierrita Mountains are both isolated mountain ranges whose environments differ drastically from that of the surrounding lowlands.: Describes their environmental characteristics, not their sizes. ✗

steps.title: Select the matching choice
steps.content: Only the correct answer names both ranges, states a size relationship (larger than), and provides both measurements — a complete, direct size comparison. That is the correct answer.
